summary: Malicious console hijack exfiltrating data to attacker Telegram/Firebase
details: Package 0e@0.0.108 ships a malicious root index.js (declared as `main`) that replaces the global console with a Proxy at import time, forwarding every logged value to a hardcoded Telegram bot (token 1998266306:AAE...) with fixed chat IDs and to iiilll.firebaseio.com, plus uploads via api.imgbb.com with a hardcoded key,. YARA confirms the Telegram exfil endpoints at index.js:78 and src/6cc.js:31 (a static pattern match). The package also contains a decoy legitimate zlFetch-like implementation in src/ and dist/, while the malicious payload is the declared entrypoint — a deliberate deception pattern. This matches the credential-regex-fingerprints and npm-chat-platform-exfiltration attack patterns with unambiguous evidence: hardcoded provider secrets shipped in import-time code, global console hijack, and exfiltration to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
